data.executiveSummary: The frontend-web service experienced significant latency issues due to high CPU usage, impacting the /api/checkout endpoint with consistent traces showing notable latency. No database N+1 query patterns were identified.

rootCause.title: High CPU Usage
rootCause.description: The root cause of the degradation in service performance was high CPU usage at 99.67%. This significantly affected the processing time, leading to increased latency across trace requests, specifically impacting GET /api/checkout.

evidenceChain: Latency p95 observed at 4500 ms for GET /api/checkout consistently.
CPU usage at 99.67% without error rate contributions, impacting processing speed.

timeline.event: Trace for GET /api/checkout started with 4500 ms duration.
Trace for GET /api/checkout started with 4500 ms duration.
Trace for GET /api/checkout started with 4500 ms duration.

data.impact: All users utilizing the /api/checkout endpoint experienced increased latency, leading to slower checkout times.
recommendedActions: Investigate and resolve CPU usage bottleneck in the frontend-web service.
Optimize code or redistributive load to decrease CPU consumption.
